key_insight: Large-step gradient descent can still be characterized and shown to converge near flat minima even when those minima form a manifold and outputs are vector-valued.
engineering_relevance: This broadens the theoretical safety zone for using aggressive learning rates in overparameterized least-squares and matrix factorization models, including settings relevant to deep learning.
method: Generalize the large-step GD normal form from scalar-output problems to vector-valued outputs.
Extend the analysis from an isolated flat minimum to a manifold of flat minima.
Use a novel method to solve a singular PDE and derive convergence theorems plus structural results for deep matrix factorization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical extensions and new structural theorems, but no experimental dataset or benchmark evaluation.
watch_item: The results rely on local neighborhood assumptions and mild structural conditions, so practical applicability may depend on verifying manifold geometry and sharpness behavior in a given model.
paper_title: Dynamics of Gradient Descent with Large Step Size Near a Manifold of Flat Minima
